review_summary: The v2 structure-first package is coherent and reproducible: discrete sign-balance is exact, orientation closure holds, weak-leakage grids are all-zero under preregistered lanes, and Lean bridge-form theorems compile in the v2 lane. The claim is supportable as a structural CP-invariance witness under declared assumptions, but not as a full continuum value derivation. The remaining risk is bridge semantics: correspondence between the discrete residual and continuum theta_QCD coefficient is still assumed rather than derived from full EFT matching.
bridge_comment: Bridge map remains conditional. Linear/affine/zero-anchored forms are theoremized, but selecting a physically unique bridge and proving UV->IR EFT correspondence is still open.
falsification_comment: Primary falsifier lanes (weak-kick/phase-shift and CKM-like transport) report zero strong-sector residual across declared grids. No blocking falsification found within declared structure-first scope.
limits: Continuum EFT matching from discrete residual to theta_QCD remains open.
Current evidence is structure-first and does not yet establish proved_core value closure.
Promotion to proved_core requires an explicit EFT identification theorem and stronger weak-sector leakage bridge proof.
